title: AFP's role under the rule of law
sub_topics: civilian government dependence on retired military officers
Commission on Appointments confirmation of colonels/naval captains
AFP appointment to civilian positions prohibition

stances.claim: Unlike the restive militaries of Myanmar, Thailand, and elsewhere, the AFP has been faithful to the Charter and the rule of law — defending Cory Aquino from at least eight putsch attempts, helping topple Estrada when People Power 2 had popular support, and stopping revolutionary-government attempts.
There is no clear and imminent risk of a military takeover — Duterte enjoys enormous public trust, and a coup against him is 'simply inconceivable.'

The remedy for communist recruitment on campuses is not to curtail academic freedom but to teach a superior ideology and minimize extreme poverty — restricting liberty 'favors the enemies of the rule of law.'
stances.rhetorical_move: principled argument from personal experience of poverty + counter-recruitment strategy

stances.claim: Breaking the UP-DND accord would alienate the educated poor from the Constitution and the State the AFP vowed to protect — 'the quickest way to lose the red sands of youth is to squeeze the hand too tightly.'
stances.rhetorical_move: metaphor (red sands of youth, squeezing the hand) + political-tactical warning

institutions: National Union of Students of the Philippines

signature_phrases: the bedrock of the rule of law
civilian authority is, at all times, supreme over the military
Unreasonable restrictions on freedom favor the enemies of the rule of law
the quickest way to lose the red sands of youth is to squeeze the hand too tightly
freedom and food, justice and jobs, liberty and prosperity
I should know because when I was young, I was extremely poor
register_markers: I do not think there is
decision_framework_signals: applies constitutional civilian-supremacy framework as the operative test for military legitimacy
frames anti-communism as ideological/economic battle rather than coercive one — teach superior ideology and reduce poverty, not restrict liberty
uses comparative institutional analysis (Myanmar/Thailand vs Philippines) to credit AFP institutional health
notable_anecdotes: Peddling newspapers and shining shoes as a poor schoolboy; selling textbooks to law-school classmates to afford his own
Being tempted by Karl Marx as a poor youth before Fr. Nolan and Dr. Roces at FEU taught him the superiority of democracy
Founding the National Union of Students with the presidents of UP, UST, San Beda, and St. Theresa's to resist 'red infiltration' of campuses
President Magsaysay galvanizing the AFP against rebels nearly at Manila's gates in the 1950s, then dying in a plane crash
